title: Mammillaria pringlei
taxonomy: Mammillaria pringlei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Mammillaria, which comprises over 200 species of cacti. The classification of Mammillaria pringlei is further divided into subgenera and sections, with some sources grouping it under the subgenus Mammillaria. The plant's classification is closely related to its morphological characteristics, such as its spines and flowers. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Mammillaria pringlei within the cactus family.
uses: Mammillaria pringlei has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating minor wounds and infections. The plant's sap is also thought to have a soothing effect on the skin, making it a potential remedy for skin irritations and rashes. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Mammillaria pringlei as a medicinal plant. Its uses should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional.

side_effects: Mammillaria pringlei is a species of cactus, and like many plants, it can cause some side effects in certain individuals. The spines of the plant can cause irritation and allergic reactions in people who come into contact with them. In rare cases, ingesting any part of the plant can cause gastrointestinal problems. Mammillaria pringlei is non-toxic, but it's still possible to experience skin irritation or allergic reactions from handling the plant.
